name: Kairos
description: Cross-platform prediction market arbitrage engine with a 5-LLM deliberation council. Scans 59K+ indexed markets across Polymarket and Kalshi, identifies genuine price discrepancies, deliberates through 5 specialized LLMs in parallel, and logs every decision permanently on Base mainnet via ERC-8004.

compute_constraints.compute_budget: 5 LLM calls per opportunity (4 parallel + 1 sequential arbiter)

council.note: Private inference — reasoning never leaves Venice AI. Only verdict is logged on-chain.

deliberation_pipeline.phase1: Four specialists analyze in parallel — each sees the opportunity data and returns a structured verdict with confidence score and reasoning
deliberation_pipeline.phase2: The Arbiter reads all four opinions plus opportunity data, synthesizes, and renders final verdict with confidence score
deliberation_pipeline.output: Full deliberation serialized and logged to Base mainnet via logDecision(market, verdict, confidence, metadata)

pre_execution_checks: Minimum spread threshold (configurable via MIN_SPREAD env var)
Council must reach verdict — no action on deliberation failure
Paper trading mode default — live execution requires explicit flag

mitigations: PR-only workflow — no direct pushes to main
Credential isolation — agent task descriptions reference env var names only
Pre-commit secret scanning via grep pattern matching
Hard rules encoded in agent permanent memory (AGENTS.md, MEMORY.md)

execution.live_requires: Set PAPER_TRADING=false and provide KAIROS_PRIVATE_KEY + KAIROS_EOA
